meaning: adj.不宜的,不顺利的,相反的,令人不快的
examples: In only one of these last eleven years was the weather unfavorable for growing citrus crops.
In contrast, unfavorable conditions that occur unpredictably pose considerable problems for organisms.
Unfavorable conditions that are relatively predictable probably pose a simpler problem for organisms than do unpredictable conditions.
The reasons behind the drop were unfavorable foreign exchange and the 20% spin-off of Zoetis.
That is, they all amount to borrowing on really, really unfavorable terms to the government.
